message:
fix(session): join background persistence threads to propagate panics

The checkpoint-save and incremental-append paths in Session::save_async
spawned background threads but never joined them, meaning a panic in the
persistence thread would silently abort without propagating to the caller.

Now captures the JoinHandle and calls handle.join() after receiving the
oneshot result, using resume_unwind to propagate any child-thread panics
back to the async caller. This ensures data-loss scenarios from panicking
serialization or filesystem operations are surfaced rather than silently
swallowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
